reasoning: With the knowledge cutoff in late October 2025 and an October 16th market probability of 36%, we are late in the year and Tesla still needs to hit a stringent 10,000 cumulative driverless (no human in car, no teleoperation), bona-fide, non-tester rides. Given typical 'Elon time' delays and the logistical/regulatory friction of scaling autonomous ride-hailing to 10k public rides, the tight timeframe suggests a strong likelihood of falling short by year-end if widespread operations haven't already hit their stride. I am slightly fading the market estimate to 33%, factoring in typical prediction market over-optimism on Tesla tech deployment timelines.
